calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-13-298079 Estate of Gerrit Mark Edris and PES-15-298462 Estate of Gerrit Mark Edris.
Appearances: Barbara Loebner & David Hoffman for Carita Klevickis & Kitsy Burdette. Charles Tate and Roy Tate in Propria Persona.

Mr. Hoffman reported that on Friday Santa Clara County appointed Ms. Klevickis as executor of the estate and that they would like her to be appointed here. Ms. Loebner reported that they have certified copies of the letters from Santa Clara County which they will file here, that the same objections were filed in Santa Clara County and dismissed by the Judge, that there is no evidence, that Carita is now the sole beneficiary and that Kitsy has been determined a full sibling. Mr. Charles Tate stated that there are remaining issues, that there are lies and perjury, that there is profound manipulation, that the typewritten will was altered from the written will, that Ms. Klevickis was added as a beneficiary, that they have offered truthful input and request to be appointed as administrator, that he would like the matter continued until the appeal is finished and that a declaration was filed this morning. Mr. Roy Tate reported that he is handling the law enforcement aspects, that his concerns were sent to the Santa Clara District Attorney, that $106,000.00 was withdrawn by William Dock, that he has been told that the 13100 withdrawal is a crime, that a zero asset probate was opened here to conceal it, that his sister is an intestate heir at law to her father's estate, that law enforcement is reviewing and forwarding to the District Attorney in San Francisco, that he has been told to contact the FBI regarding the fraud, he asks that the court does not appoint Ms. Klevickis and requests that the court appoint his brother (Charles Tate) or to postpone the matter due to the appeal. Mr. Hoffman stated that Charles Tate appealed the order from July, that the arguments today were also made on Friday, that his client is 90 years old, that no one from the DA or FBI has contacted him regarding this, and that there is good and clear reason to appoint his client. Ms. Loebner stated that it is silly to respond to their accusations, that there is confusion regarding the 13100, that there was clearly an account with Mr. Edris name on it that was thought to be in the trust, that the trust has no beneficiaries, that there was a clerical error made on the will, that the Judge stated that it does not change the intent of the will, that they did not offer money to Kitsy, that the 13100 was filled out prior to the 40 day lapse, that the money was transferred to Clarita's bank account and she was instructed to leave it intact, that Mr. Dock did not understand the law and she explained how Charles & Roy came to be notified of the matter. Mr. Roy Tate stated that he has an email with an offer & response regarding money offered to Kitsy, that he wants the checks, that he has now been told that the 13100 with Union Bank was back dated and that it is comical to say that Bill Dock did not understand. Mr. Charles Tate stated that Ms. Loebner is assigning all discrepancies, errors and mistakes as inconveniences but that it is truly an attempt to defraud himself and his brother. The Court stated that once it has received the copies of the certified letters the petitions for letters of administration in both cases will be submitted.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
